voters.comment: Hopefully Ola isn't the only one who votes for this lol probably my last Lana Del Rey submission anyway, since she usually doesn't do very well in votes, but I just love this song

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I enjoyed the story behind this one. Despite Lana Del Ray usually having a very different sound, I can definitely hear the psychedelic influence in this one.

voters.name: Ola
voters.comment: I almost submitted lana del rey this round! but couldn't decide on a single song -- to me, it's a full album listen. specifically, if you're doing it like me, "born to die" on repeat with black coffee and topology homework in the wee hours.

voters.name: Dorothy
voters.comment: Gotta have some Lana Del Ray in 2010

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: ohhh I love it. Never listened to much more of their stuff, but I might now.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: Red Jumpsuit played at WWWY last month at the *exact same time* as Arm's Length (my submission for this week) so I unfortunately had to miss their set. But I was real tempted to skip out early and check out their stage because I love this song!

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Instantly knew the song but I don't think I've ever known the title and artist lol.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Cool story, they're a Christian rock band. In fact, this song charted number one on Christian stations. The song In Fate's Hands was originally called AssShaker though so...
